classical_baseline.note: a per-feature angle-encoding Ry(x_i) product map is a genuine distance-sensitive kernel: overlap falls off smoothly as ||x-y|| grows, unlike a degenerate constant map that reports the same kernel for every pair

meta.author: worked-example
meta.note: product-state overlap factorizes per qubit as cos^2((scale*(x_i-y_i))/2); the visible near-pair (Delta ~ 0.05 per feature) lands at kernel ~0.9988 (>= 0.9), the held-out far-pair (Delta ~2.5-2.8 per feature) lands at kernel ~0.0029 (<= 0.1) -- the SAME template genuinely distinguishes both, no ancilla/SWAP-test register needed since the judge re-simulates x and y independently and compares the two statevectors directly
